claim: Provider isolation is implemented but has gaps: agent_tool_profile field is set but never used by provider adapter, provider override bypass risk is mitigated by adapter-level normalization, and cross-provider tool cleanup is provider-scoped not comprehensive.

evidence.summary: Provider adapter only reads Claude-specific fields (claude_permission_mode, settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools, claude_disable_slash_commands, claude_no_session_persistence, max_budget_usd) and does NOT read agent_tool_profile.
evidence.why: This contradicts the explorer's risk claim about parameter rejection - the field is silently ignored not rejected.

evidence.summary: Line 462 sets ai.agent_tool_profile = normalizedProfile, but provider-adapter.ts never reads this field.
evidence.why: This confirms the field is dead configuration that creates a false sense of tool control.

evidence.summary: selectedProvider function ALWAYS calls normalizeAgentProvider(provider) before using provider value, providing adapter-level normalization even if evidence-dispatch bypass doesn't normalize.
evidence.why: This validates a defense-in-depth pattern that mitigates the provider override bypass risk.

evidence.summary: Line 127 calls selectedProvider(detectModelProvider()) which normalizes provider at spawn time, providing second normalization checkpoint.
evidence.why: This shows double normalization protection: at buildProviderInvocation and at spawnProviderPrompt.

evidence.summary: Provider-specific cleanup is provider-scoped: Claude tool fields cleared only when provider is claude (lines 457-459), Claude model cleared only when provider is codex (lines 447-449), custom sandbox set only when provider is custom (lines 464-465).
evidence.why: This confirms incomplete cross-provider cleanup - each provider only cleans its own specific fields, not all provider-specific fields.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider function implements PROVIDER_ALIASES mapping and returns null for auto, providing the normalization foundation.
evidence.why: This validates the explorer's claim about normalization foundation.

evidence.summary: Test verifies Codex dispatch does not inherit Claude default model and checks provider isolation at config level.
evidence.why: This confirms the explorer's test evidence about provider isolation validation.

evidence.summary: Boundary tests verify project mutation detection and blocking for both tracked and untracked files.
evidence.why: This validates the boundary enforcement claim with concrete test evidence.

evidence.summary: Boundary enforcement implementation with buildBoundarySnapshot before (line 516), diff after (line 567), and violation detection (line 569).
evidence.why: This confirms the boundary enforcement mechanism works as claimed.

assumptions: Silent ignoring of unrecognized ai fields like agent_tool_profile is acceptable behavior rather than a configuration bug.
Provider-scoped cleanup is sufficient even though cross-provider configuration leakage is theoretically possible.
Adapter-level normalization at spawn time compensates for lack of normalization at config build time.
risks: agent_tool_profile field creates false confidence about tool capability control since it is never actually used.
Provider-scoped cleanup means if a provider receives another provider's configuration fields, those fields won't be cleared.
Defense-in-depth normalization pattern may hide the root cause of unnormalized provider strings reaching config building.
Cross-provider tool configuration leakage could occur if provider isolation fails and fields are not comprehensively cleared.
missing: Evidence that Claude CLI silently ignores agent_tool_profile without warnings or errors.
Test coverage for cross-provider configuration leakage scenarios.
Verification that Codex/custom providers don't have equivalent tool profile configuration needs.

result.notes: Cross-check revealed agent_tool_profile is dead configuration (set but never used), provider override bypass risk is mitigated by adapter-level normalization, and cross-provider cleanup is incomplete but provider-scoped. Require clarification on whether agent_tool_profile should be removed or implemented.
